Memo to Helen
Subject: I am so sorry — please read

Helen,

I have to start by apologizing. I've made a mess of this week and I want to take full responsibility before you read any further. I'm so sorry. I should have been better.

The finance figure on Friday's status. I cannot tell you how sorry I am about this. I noticed it Friday afternoon and I just — I should have caught it earlier, I should have flagged it, I should have done something. The fact that it went out to the sponsor and to M. Whitfield's group with the wrong number is on me and I am genuinely losing sleep over it. I sent a correction Monday but I keep thinking about how that landed in their inboxes on Friday afternoon and how the sponsor read the wrong number going into the weekend. I'm so sorry. I know you signed it off but it's still my responsibility and I let you down. I should have triple-checked.

I want you to know I have been beating myself up about this all week. I went into Monday morning already exhausted from worrying about it.

The vendor — T. Reyes missed the integration plan. I emailed her Monday but she didn't reply until Wednesday. I'm sorry I couldn't get her to move faster. I tried. I'm not sure what else I could have done.

M. Whitfield emailed about split-jurisdiction reporting. I responded but I'm worried I didn't say the right thing. I asked R. Silva to send him an analysis on Tuesday. I'm sorry I had to bother him while you were away.

Raj and Priya couldn't start their work because the integration plan didn't arrive. I'm sorry I couldn't fix that. I told them you'd be back soon.

I'm so sorry for the disappointing week. I'll do better. Please let me know if there's anything I should be doing differently. I'm really, really sorry about the finance figure.

Coordinator
